_meta.purpose: Map iw3sp_mod.exe -> iw3mp.exe addresses for porting iw3sp_mod gamepad subsystem to CoD4x_Client_pub

vt_session.name: CoD4_SP_vs_MP
vt_session.created_by: user via Ghidra GUI Version Tracking
vt_session.matchers_applied: Exact Function Instructions, Exact Function Mnemonics, Combined Function and Data Reference, Implied Match (auto-derived), plus more

_meta.computation_rule: iw3mp_addr = iw3mp_function_entry + offset_in_function -- VALID ONLY for ACCEPTED matches where 'Exact Function Instructions' confirms byte-identical bodies. AVAILABLE/Implied matches need byte-level verification before trusting the offset.

active_hooks.vt_algo: Stage2Finish.java string-fingerprint (4/4 strings shared); offset rejected by V2 byte-verify
active_hooks._note: Function identified by 4/4 strings (PLATFORM_LOW_AMMO_NO_RELOAD, PLATFORM_RELOAD, WEAPON_NO_AMMO, 'low ammo warning'). Offset 0xE0 INVALID in iw3mp (lands on non-instruction; sizes 641 vs 687 differ). Need byte-pattern search inside 0x4237B0 for the sequence around iw3sp 0x40A0B0: '20 60 68 00 E8 77 E8 FF FF 8B 0D ?? ?? ?? ?? EB' -- specifically the `push 0x680020; call rel32; mov ecx,[mem]; jmp` shape.

active_hooks.vt_algo: Stage2Finish.java fingerprint (insn count exact: 270==270, string match)
active_hooks._note: STRONG match: instruction count identical (270), sizes 951 vs 970, shared string '%f : %f\n'. This is a call-direct (function entry), so iw3mp_addr = function entry. Critical for Stage 3D port.

active_hooks.vt_algo: Stage2FinishV2.java string-fingerprint (5/5 strings shared, top candidate by far)
active_hooks._note: Re-confirmed after V2: all 5 unique strings shared ('+attack', '%s is unbound, use controls menu to set.', '%s %i %i\n', '-%s %i %i\n', 'devgui'). Earlier byte-level REJECT (V1) was due to register-allocation differences between SP and MP builds (first insn: MOV EDX vs MOV EAX), not wrong function. iw3mp body is 11% larger (1261 vs 1399 insns 381 vs 417) due to MP-specific paths. Since hook is a direct CALL to function entry, only the entry matters: 0x467EB0.

Discovered via caller search: iw3sp CL_MouseMove (0x43FA90) has exactly 1 caller -> host 0x4402B0 (call site 0x4402F7). iw3mp CL_MouseMove (0x463490) also has exactly 1 caller -> host 0x463D10 (call site 0x463D70). Source offset 0x47 differs from dest offset 0x60 because MP has a different prologue (size 188 vs 238), but the SEMANTIC hook site (the call to CL_MouseMove) is 0x463D70. Hook this address directly.

active_hooks.vt_algo: Implied Match REJECTED by Stage2Finish.java byte verify
active_hooks._note: REJECTED: implied target 0x4EAAD0 had different size (86 vs 107), different insn count (22 vs 32), and diverging mnemonics from insn 2. Wrong function. Needs alternate discovery.
Promoted from implied: 21-byte common prefix, 24 mnemonics identical, sizes 137 vs 147 close (relocations explain the diff). Trust offset 0x3F.

active_hooks.vt_algo: Combined Function and Data Reference Match
active_hooks._note: Very high confidence (1710.984) -- strongest match in the set.
UI_Refresh not matched. Needs alternate matching.

Promoted from implied: 24 mnemonics identical, sizes 335 vs 338 close (only ~1% diff). prefixBytes=0 because head has relocated CALL targets, but structural fingerprint matches. CRITICAL: root of XInput polling -- trust offset 0x93.

V2 confirmed: 0x5947A8 sits in a gap between iw3sp prev function ending ~0x594729 and next function 0x5947F0 -- Ghidra never created a function here. Disassembly at 0x5947A8: MOV ECX,[ESP+0x14]; CALL 0x43F920; ADD ESP,0xC; TEST EAX,EAX; MOV [0x13E3FEC],EAX; JZ 0x5947E0. So 0x43F920 is the callee (likely IN_MouseEvent equivalent at ~CL_MouseMove-0xD0). To find iw3mp equivalent: locate iw3mp's equivalent of 0x43F920, find its single caller-block, hook the equivalent of +0x8 (relative to call site).
